name: output
description: Check background work progress. For a running work it returns status plus the tail of its stdout/stderr so far (output_tail, bounded); the full result still arrives as a message when it finishes. This is for an occasional peek only — do NOT call it in a loop and do NOT run `sleep` to wait for completion. Ending your turn is safe: the finished result arrives as a message that automatically wakes you.

handle.description: The work handle returned when the background command started.
